items.title: Hugging Face Discloses Autonomous AI-Agent-Driven Production Breach

claims.claim: Breach driven end-to-end by autonomous AI agent framework; detected July 16
Attack vector: remote-code dataset loader and template injection in dataset configuration
Campaign executed 17,000+ automated actions via short-lived sandboxes over a weekend
Impact: limited internal datasets and service credentials; public models/supply chain clean

caveats: Partner/customer data exposure assessment ongoing; final scope not published
17,000+ events figure from Hugging Face's own LLM-triage pipeline; methodology not independently verified

items.title: Claude Code v2.1.212 Adds /fork, Session Resource Caps, and MCP Auto-Background

claims.claim: /fork copies conversation to new background session; /subtask replaces in-session fork
WebSearch per-session cap: 200 default; CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_WEB_SEARCHES_PER_SESSION env var
Subagent spawn cap: 200 default; CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_SUBAGENTS_PER_SESSION; resets on /clear
MCP tool auto-background after 2 minutes; configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_MCP_AUTO_BACKGROUND_MS
/resume opens past-session picker including deleted sessions
48 total changes including Windows PowerShell 7 preference, /ultrareview PR-ref fix, prompt caching fixes

items.title: Google Renames NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, Adds Code Execution for 30M Users

claims.claim: Renamed to Gemini Notebook July 16; serves 30M+ users and 600,000+ orgs
Code execution (secure cloud computer) live for AI Ultra and Workspace AI Ultra/Expanded Access users
Pro user code execution rollout: coming weeks, no specific date
Gemini 3.5 + Antigravity upgrade expanding from Ultra to all AI Pro users on the web
Notebook sync across Gemini app; Google Search AI Mode integration soon

caveats: Pro code execution rollout timing unspecified
Gemini Search AI Mode integration is forward-looking, not yet shipped
Antigravity upgrade detail from 9to5Google, not directly in primary blog post

items.title: China's Dual AI Regulation Enforcement: Agent Governance and Companion Rules Take Effect

claims.claim: Implementation Opinions on Intelligent Agent Governance enforceable July 15; world's first dedicated AI agent regulatory framework
Three-tier decision authorization: agent actions classified by consequence level; sensitive sectors require filing
Interim Measures for AI Anthropomorphic Interactive Services enforceable July 15; covers emotional AI companions
Companion rules: mandates distress detection, crisis intervention, bans addictive design; data-use restrictions
ByteDance and Alibaba disabled companion AI features ahead of deadline
Penalties: CNY 100,000 basic; CNY 200,000 for citizen health/safety harm

caveats: Chinese-language primary government texts not fetched; English sources used
No enforcement actions reported as of July 15; this reflects rules taking effect

items.title: Altman, Amodei, Hassabis Converge on Mandatory Pre-Release Model Scrutiny

claims.claim: Altman, Amodei, Hassabis all endorse mandatory pre-release scrutiny for frontier models
Amodei: FAA-model federal agency with day-1 release-blocking power
Hassabis: FINRA-model industry-funded body, voluntary to mandatory escalation
Altman: IAEA-model U.S.-led international forum using market access as compliance leverage

caveats: Based on public statements; no primary legislative proposals or formal agreements published
Three proposals are mutually incompatible on enforcement mechanism

items.title: Anthropic Posts 32 Enforcement Analyst Roles in Largest Safety Hiring Push

claims.claim: 32 concurrent enforcement analyst job postings; specializations: radiological/nuclear, chemical/explosives, financial scams, cybercrime
Pay range: mid-to-upper $200Ks
Spokesperson: stress-test systems and bolster defenses before a model ever goes live

caveats: Job postings not independently fetched; Axios reporting with spokesperson confirmation
Hiring signal, not confirmed hires; no policy change to Claude's existing constraints implied

items.title: WAICO Founded: 29 Countries Sign Agreement in Shanghai

claims.claim: 29 countries signed WAICO founding agreement in Shanghai on July 16
Chinese FM Wang Yi signed for China; UN SG Guterres attended
Named founding members include Russia, Kazakhstan, Pakistan, Indonesia, Laos
Xi Jinping keynote July 17: called WAICO an important milestone; pledged 5,000 AI training opportunities to developing nations

items.update_note: July 17 bulletin treated WAICO as unconfirmed proposal; signing confirmed by Xinhua T2 source
caveats: Full list of 29 countries not published in English-language primary sources
WAICO charter and operational details not yet published
